title: Ensure 'Versioning' is set to 'Enabled' on Azure Blob  Storage storage accounts

input_row.title: Ensure 'Versioning' is set to 'Enabled' on Azure Blob  Storage storage accounts
input_row.description: Enabling blob versioning allows for the automatic retention of previous versions of objects. With blob versioning enabled, earlier versions of a blob are accessible for data recovery in the event of modifications or deletions.
input_row.rationale: Blob versioning safeguards data integrity and enables recovery by retaining previous versions of stored objects, facilitating quick restoration from accidental deletion, modification, or malicious activity.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account with blob storage. 3. In the Overview page, on the Properties tab, under Blob service, ensure Versioning is set to Enabled. 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each storage account with blob storage. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list storage accounts: az storage account list Run the following command to determine if a storage account has containers: az storage container list --account-name For each storage account with containers, ensure that the output of the below command contains "isVersioningEnabled": true: az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list storage accounts: Get-AzStorageAccount Run the following command to create an Azure Storage context for a storage account: $context = New-AzStorageContext -StorageAccountName Run the following command to list containers for the storage account: Get-AzStorageContainer -Context $context If the storage account has containers, run the following command to get the blob service properties of the storage account: $account = Get-AzStorageBlobServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName -AccountName Run the following command to get the blob versioning setting for the storage account: $account.IsVersioningEnabled Ensure that the command returns True. Repeat for each storage account. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c36a325b-ae04-4863-ad4f-19c6678f8e08 - Name: 'Configure your Storage account to enable blob versioning'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account with blob storage. 3. In the Overview page, on the Properties tab, under Blob service, click Disabled next to Versioning. 4. Under Tracking, check the box next to Enable versioning for blobs. 5. Select the radio button next to Keep all versions or Delete versions after (in days). 6. If selecting to delete versions, enter a number of in the box after which to delete blob versions. 7. Click Save. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each storage account with blob storage. Remediate from Azure CLI For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to enable blob versioning: az storage account blob-service-properties update --account-name --enable-versioning true Remediate from PowerShell For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to enable blob versioning: Update-AzStorageBlobServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName - StorageAccountName -IsVersioningEnabled $true
input_row.impact: Enabling blob versioning for a storage account creates a new version with each write operation to a blob, which can increase storage costs. To control these costs, a lifecycle management policy can be applied to automatically delete older versions.
input_row.references: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account/blob-service- properties 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageaccount 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/new- azstoragecontext 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstoragecontainer 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstorageblobserviceproperty 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/update- azstorageblobserviceproperty 8. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/versioning-overview 9. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/lifecycle-management- overview
input_row.default_value: Blob versioning is disabled by default on storage accounts.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified via AWS APIs/boto3 and AWS CLI. AWS provides APIs to manage S3 objects, including versioning. The description and remediation suggest clear automation paths through CLI commands and API methods to both audit and remediate the configuration.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_s3_bucket_versioning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use AWS SDK (boto3) `s3.list_buckets()` to get all S3 buckets in the account.
2. For each bucket, call `s3.get_bucket_versioning(Bucket='bucket_name')` to check the versioning status.
3. Validate that the returned output has a key `Status` with value `Enabled` to confirm S3 bucket versioning is enabled. 

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- Pass: If the `Status` key in the response is `Enabled`.
- Fail: If `Status` is `Suspended` or the key does not exist, implying versioning is not enabled.

With this approach, all properties regarding the versioning of the S3 buckets can be efficiently queried and validated across the environment.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is correct because the status of S3 bucket versioning can be checked using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI:** The control can indeed be checked via AWS APIs/boto3/CLI. However, the original control is for Azure Blob Storage, not AWS S3. The audit approach should be aligned with AWS services, but the control description is mismatched.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- **Automation Details:** The boto3 methods `s3.list_buckets()` and `s3.get_bucket_versioning(Bucket='bucket_name')` are valid for checking S3 bucket versioning status.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_s3_bucket_versioning_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity. It should reflect the check more precisely, such as `aws_s3_bucket_versioning_status_check`.
- **Accuracy:** The technical steps are correct for AWS S3, but the control description is for Azure Blob Storage, which is a mismatch. The audit needs to be aligned with AWS services.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Control Description Alignment:** Ensure that the control description matches the AWS service being audited. The current control is for Azure Blob Storage, which is not applicable to AWS S3.
- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_s3_bucket_versioning_status_check` for better clarity.
- **Additional Considerations:** Include error handling in the automation script to manage cases where the bucket does not exist or the API call fails.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- The technical steps for AWS S3 are correct, but the control description mismatch lowers confidence in the overall assessment.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Update the control description to align with AWS S3 services instead of Azure Blob Storage. Ensure that the audit is relevant to AWS.
- **Re-classify:** The approach remains Automated, but the control description needs to be corrected to reflect AWS services accurately.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Despite the original control description being for Azure Blob Storage, the concept of versioning is applicable to AWS S3, which can be audited using AWS APIs. Both AI opinions agree that AWS APIs, specifically boto3, can be used to check the versioning status of S3 buckets. The audit can be automated by leveraging AWS SDK methods to ensure versioning is enabled, aligning with the control's intent of safeguarding data integrity.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_s3_bucket_versioning_status_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** S3 Buckets
- **API/CLI Method:** boto3 `s3.list_buckets()`, `s3.get_bucket_versioning(Bucket='bucket_name')`
- **Properties to Check:** `Status` field in the response of `get_bucket_versioning`
- **Pass Criteria:** `Status` is `Enabled`
- **Fail Criteria:** `Status` is `Suspended` or the `Status` key does not exist

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** The second AI opinion highlighted a mismatch between the control's original Azure context and AWS services. However, the underlying principle of versioning is applicable to AWS S3, and the automation approach is technically feasible with AWS APIs, justifying the decision to automate.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Microsoft_Azure_Foundations_Benchmark_v5_0_0
